# Correlation rule — failed-logon brute force (T1110)

**Suggested severity**: Medium · **Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Scheduled correlation rule: >20 failed logons per source-IP/account in a 10-minute window. `bin _time span = 10m` buckets time; `comp count() as fails ... by _time, action_remote_ip, auth_identity` aggregates per window per entity; `filter fails > 20` is the threshold. Project the entity columns (`action_remote_ip`, `auth_identity`, host) so the generated alert is enrichable. NOTE: `auth_outcome` is the IdP/cloud-auth outcome field (value `"FAILURE"`) — populated for identity/SaaS logins, often NULL for raw endpoint logons, so confirm population on your tenant. Set schedule=Every 10 min / window=10 min, Severity=Medium, MITRE=T1110 in the rule wrapper (not the XQL).

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.LOGIN_EVENT and auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| fields _time, action_remote_ip, auth_identity, agent_hostname
| bin _time span = 10m
| comp count() as fails, count_distinct(agent_hostname) as targets by _time, action_remote_ip, auth_identity
| filter fails > 20
| fields _time, action_remote_ip, auth_identity, fails, targets
```

_Correlation-rule XQL body live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (runs to SUCCESS with confirmed fields). The schedule, time window, threshold-tuning, severity, MITRE mapping and suppression are rule-wrapper settings in the XSIAM editor — not in the XQL. See the `cortex_detection_rule_authoring` skill._
